rulings.Case Title: SHAWN-MICHAEL MATHIES VS. UNION BANK, N.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 11, 2013, Line 23, PLAINTIFF SHAWN-MICHAEL MATHIES Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery. Grant. Plaintiff's motion is timely. The email chain pertaining to percentages relates to the reclassification project regardless of the emails being sent after reclassification. Within 10 days, Defendant shall produce Ms. Omoto's emails with members of the task force regarding assigned percentages of time for tasks and draft descriptions for the CSM position after reclassification. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MELINDA BARANY et al VS. MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 11, 2013, Line 1. Plaintiffs MELINDA BARANY and ALBERT KUN'S Motion to Compel Production of Documents Pro Tem Judge Élan Lambert, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TIM ROBINSON INDIVIDUALLY, AND WHORL LLC, VS. COOLEY GODWARD KRONISH LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Fmtn And Mtn For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 11, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANTs PETER WERNER, KENNETH GUERNSEY, BARRY GRAYNOR, CRAIG JACOBY and COOLEY LLP's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Judge Miller discloses and will disclose at the hearing that she is a member of the same Inn of Court as Mr. Tangri, and that Mr. Tangri was an associate for some period of time when she was a director at the former Howard, Rice firm. Judge Miller does not recall when Mr. Tangri was at the firm, except that it was some time before December 1995, which is when Judge Miller left the firm. Judge Miller is on the board of ABTL, Northern California chapter, and Daralyn Durie is also a board member. None of this will affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. The Court's tentative ruling is to grant the motion, for good cause. No opposition was filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 11, 2013, Line 2. Defendants JORGE BARRIENTOS and WEM PACIFIC INVESTMENT INC.'S Motion to Compel Responses to Request for Production of Documents (Set 1) and for Monetary Sanctions as Against Plaintiff and His Attorney of Record Keith Oliver Esq in the Amount of $810.00 Pro Tem Judge Élan Lambert, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted defendant's motion to compel responses to request for documents pursuant to the parties stipulation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to elan.lambert@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WAYNE TAM VS. VAN NESS AUTO BODY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 11, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT HUIZHEN YUAN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Huizhen ("Sammi") Yuan's demurrer to the first cause of action (intentional infliction of emotional distress) and third cause of action (negligence) is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to state a cause of action. See CCP 430.10(e). The causes of action as to Defendant Yuan are solely based on the allegation that she is liable for calling the police on Plaintiff. This conduct is absolutely privileged under Hagberg v. California Federal Bank FSB (2004) 32 Cal.4th 350. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LAUREE PRIETO VS. B&H EDUCATION INC. DBA MARINELLO SCHOOLS OF BEAUTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 11, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT B&H EDUCATION, INC. DBA MARINELLO SCHOOLS OF BEAUTY'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions. The Court has received the correspondence of both counsel dated June 7, 2013 indicating all of the areas of agreement with regard to discovery and the tentative ruling issued on May 29, 2013. The Court will adopt the areas of agreement as the ruling of the Court. As to the areas on which the parties did not reach agreement, the Court rules as follows: Requests for Production 2, 8, and 22: the Court reiterates its earlier tentative ruling that within 20 days, Plaintiff shall provide verified further responses and produce documents. If there are document requests where Plaintiff has conducted a reasonably diligent search and has by now produced all responsive, non-privileged documents in her possession, custody or control, she should state so in writing. For any responsive document withheld on the basis of privilege, responding party shall serve a privilege log that identifies the nature of the document, date, author, recipients, Bates numbers, and the privilege claimed. Special interrogatory No. 17: the Court reiterates its earlier tentative ruling that Plaintiff shall respond to this interrogatory. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 11, 2013, Line 10, PLAINTIFF LAUREE PRIETO's Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions. The hearing is continued to June 24, 2013 so that the parties can further meet and confer on the discovery that is the subject of this motion. The parties are ordered to meet and confer, and must do so in person or on the telephone, rather than by the exchange of letters or emails. By no later than June 20, 2013, the parties are to provide the Court a written update, identifying by request number those document requests in this motion, if any, that are still in dispute between them. This may be provided to the Court in the form of a letter or a short brief no more than 3 pages. There will be no additional argument in this written update. A courtesy copy of the written update must be delivered to the Court by 4:00 p.m. on June 20, 2013, clearly stating that it is to be brought to the Court's attention for the June 24, 2013 calendar. If the June 24, 2013 hearing date is not mutually convenient to the parties, the parties must meet and confer on another suitable date and be prepared to address this at the hearing on June 11, 2013. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DIVYA CHABDLER VS. GREGG JOHNSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 11, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT GREGG JOHNSON, MOLLY JOHNSON DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained. Plaintiff's claim for negligent misrepresentation is barred by the two year statute of limitations. See Ventura Cnty. Nat. Bank v. Macker, 49 Cal. App. 4th 1528, 1531 (1996); Loken v. Century 21-Award Properties (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 263. Plaintiff discovered the truth about the defects in the fireplace and the electrical system in or around December 2009, but did not file the present claim until September 7, 2012. Consequently, the claim is barred because it was filed more than two years after Plaintiff learned the truth about the defects in the property. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK FSB A FEDERAL SAVINGS BANK VS. JAVIER VALLIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Deem Matters Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions; Ntc Of Submission On Pltff'S Mtn To Deem Matters Admitted; Statement Of Facts
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 11, 2013, Line 4. Plaintiff AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK FSB'S Motion to Deem Matters Admitted and for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Elan Lambert, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. Moving party failed to provide courtesy copies as required by Local Rule 2.6B and the Court's order issued on June 3, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to elan.lambert@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD J. CURIALE VS. RBZ LLP, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion For An Order Transferring Venue To Los Angeles County
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 11, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANT RBZ LLP Motion For An Order Transferring Venue To Los Angeles County. Denied in its entirety. Defendant has failed to meet its burden to show that venue is improper in San Francisco. Venue is proper in San Francisco. See CCP §395.5. For example, the contracts between the parties were entered into in San Francisco during the relevant period, where it was reviewed and signed by the firm. (Curiale Decl. 3 and 5.) The alleged injuries to Plaintiff occurred in San Francisco, where he is located. The requests for attorneys fees and costs are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
